registry.description: Worth is the intrinsic value of something — what it is actually worth, not what the market assigns to it or what flattery claims for it. The Hebrew vocabulary here covers both the likeness or comparison that establishes value and the formal valuation that sets a price. In Scripture, worth is ultimately theological: the worth of a human being derives from being made in the image of God and known by name. Worth is also relational: to be worthy of something is to be fittingly matched to it.

adv
1) like, as, the like of which
conj
2) when, according as, as it were

terms.medium_def:
A: 1) (relative part.)
1a) which, who
1b) that which
2) (conj)
2a) that (in obj clause)
2b) when
2c) since
2d) as
2e) conditional if
B: Beth+ 1) in (that) which
2) (adv)
2a) where
3) (conj)
3a) in that, inasmuch as
3b) on account of
C: Mem+ 1) from (or than) that which
2) from (the place) where
3) from (the fact) that, since
D: Kaph+ 
1) (conj.), according as, as, when
1a) according to that which, according as, as
1b) with a causal force: in so far as, since
1c) with a temporal force: when

and awakes faint, with his thirst not quenched,
so shall the multitude of all the nations be
that fight against Mount Zion.

verses.esv_text: Dan 2:24 Therefore Daniel went in to Arioch , whom the king had appointed to destroy the wise men of Babylon . He went and said thus to him: “Do not destroy the wise men of Babylon ; bring me in before the king , and I will show the king the interpretation .”

verses.esv_text: Dan 2:9 if you do not make the dream known to me, there is but one sentence for you. You have agreed to speak lying and corrupt words before me till the times change . Therefore tell me the dream , and I shall know that you can show me its interpretation .”

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:1 King Nebuchadnezzar made an image of gold , whose height was sixty cubits and its breadth six cubits . He set it up on the plain of Dura , in the province of Babylon .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:1 King Nebuchadnezzar made an image of gold, whose height was sixty cubits and its breadth six cubits. He set it up on the plain of Dura, in the province of Babylon.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:10 You , O king , have made a decree , that every man who hears the sound of the horn , pipe , lyre , trigon , harp , bagpipe , and every kind of music , shall fall down and worship the golden image .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:10 You, O king, have made a decree, that every man who hears the sound of the horn, pipe, lyre, trigon, harp, bagpipe, and every kind of music, shall fall down and worship the golden image.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:11 And whoever does not fall down and worship shall be cast into a burning fiery furnace .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:11 And whoever does not fall down and worship shall be cast into a burning fiery furnace.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:12 There are certain Jews whom you have appointed over the affairs of the province of Babylon : Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego . These men , O king , pay no attention to you ; they do not serve your gods or worship the golden image that you have set up .”
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:12 There are certain Jews whom you have appointed over the affairs of the province of Babylon: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego. These men, O king, pay no attention to you; they do not serve your gods or worship the golden image that you have set up.”

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:14 Nebuchadnezzar answered and said to them, “Is it true , O Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego , that you do not serve my gods or worship the golden image that I have set up ?
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:14 Nebuchadnezzar answered and said to them, “Is it true, O Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, that you do not serve my gods or worship the golden image that I have set up?

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:15 Now if you are ready when you hear the sound of the horn , pipe , lyre , trigon , harp , bagpipe , and every kind of music , to fall down and worship the image that I have made , well and good. But if you do not worship , you shall immediately be cast into a burning fiery furnace . And who is the god who will deliver you out of my hands ?”
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:15 Now if you are ready when you hear the sound of the horn, pipe, lyre, trigon, harp, bagpipe, and every kind of music, to fall down and worship the image that I have made, well and good. But if you do not worship, you shall immediately be cast into a burning fiery furnace. And who is the god who will deliver you out of my hands?”

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:17 If this be so, our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace , and he will deliver us out of your hand , O king .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:17 If this be so, our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace, and he will deliver us out of your hand, O king.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:18 But if not , be it known to you, O king , that we will not serve your gods or worship the golden image that you have set up .”
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:18 But if not, be it known to you, O king, that we will not serve your gods or worship the golden image that you have set up.”

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:19 Then Nebuchadnezzar was filled with fury , and the expression of his face was changed against Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego . He ordered the furnace heated seven times more than it was usually heated .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:19 Then Nebuchadnezzar was filled with fury, and the expression of his face was changed against Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego. He ordered the furnace heated seven times more than it was usually heated.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:2 Then King Nebuchadnezzar sent to gather the satraps , the prefects , and the governors , the counselors , the treasurers , the justices , the magistrates , and all the officials of the provinces to come to the dedication of the image that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:2 Then King Nebuchadnezzar sent to gather the satraps, the prefects, and the governors, the counselors, the treasurers, the justices, the magistrates, and all the officials of the provinces to come to the dedication of the image that King Nebuchadnezzar had set up.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:20 And he ordered some of the mighty men of his army to bind Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego , and to cast them into the burning fiery furnace .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:20 And he ordered some of the mighty men of his army to bind Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, and to cast them into the burning fiery furnace.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:22 Because the king’s order was urgent and the furnace overheated , the flame of the fire killed those men who took up Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:22 Because the king’s order was urgent and the furnace overheated, the flame of the fire killed those men who took up Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:25 He answered and said , “ But I see four men unbound , walking in the midst of the fire , and they are not hurt ; and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods .”
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:25 He answered and said, “But I see four men unbound, walking in the midst of the fire, and they are not hurt; and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods.”

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:26 Then Nebuchadnezzar came near to the door of the burning fiery furnace ; he declared , “ Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego , servants of the Most High God , come out , and come here !” Then Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego came out from the fire .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:26 Then Nebuchadnezzar came near to the door of the burning fiery furnace; he declared, “Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, servants of the Most High God, come out, and come here!” Then Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego came out from the fire.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:27 And the satraps , the prefects , the governors , and the king’s counselors gathered together and saw that the fire had not had any power over the bodies of those men . The hair of their heads was not singed , their cloaks were not harmed , and no smell of fire had come upon them .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:27 And the satraps, the prefects, the governors, and the king’s counselors gathered together and saw that the fire had not had any power over the bodies of those men. The hair of their heads was not singed, their cloaks were not harmed, and no smell of fire had come upon them.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:28 Nebuchadnezzar answered and said , “ Blessed be the God of Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego , who has sent his angel and delivered his servants , who trusted in him, and set aside the king’s command , and yielded up their bodies rather than serve and worship any god except their own God .
verses.preview_html: Dan 3:28 Nebuchadnezzar answered and said, “Blessed be the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego, who has sent his angel and delivered his servants, who trusted in him, and set aside the king’s command, and yielded up their bodies rather than serve and worship any god except their own God.

verses.esv_text: Dan 3:29 Therefore I make a decree : Any people , nation , or language that speaks anything against the God of Shadrach , Meshach , and Abednego shall be torn limb from limb , and their houses laid in ruins , for there is no other god who is able to rescue in this way.”

terms.medium_def:
1) ephah
1a) a dry measure of quantity, equal to 3 seahs, 10 omers; the same as the liquid measure bath; (about 9 imperial gallons (40 l), rabbinical writings give sizes of one-half this amount)
1b) the receptacle for measuring or holding that amount
